22: ...If I am not mistaken, you are an adventurer of some prestige, yes? Have you ever given any thought to becoming a mentor?
Ah, I was expecting you! The Adventurers' Guild was asked to assist with the new training courses for the Eorzean Alliance.

23: The role of a mentor is to provide guidance and support for beginning adventurers. Pray speak with me further should you have an interest in helping us mold the next generation of heroes!

I notice you have yet to complete all of the trials on offer. If I may be so bold, I strongly recommend doing so, as the knowledge gained from them will surely pay off, no matter where the fair winds take you.

24:
By the gods! You...you're <Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>! What might this humble Smith do to aid you this day?
...Or mayhap it is aid you have come to offer?
Allow me to commend you on your mastery of our tactical training. I pray these advanced strategies will be of use to you in the battles yet to come!

25: We of the Adventurers' Guild are always on the lookout for seasoned veterans who are willing to assume the role of “mentor.”
...If I am not mistaken, you are an adventurer of some prestige, yes? Have you ever given any thought to becoming a mentor?

26: The knowledge and experience you have accumulated over your illustrious career would be of great benefit to those just starting out in the adventuring profession. Without a doubt, you have the makings of a marvelous mentor! What say you?
The role of a mentor is to provide guidance and support for beginning adventurers. Pray speak with me further should you have an interest in helping us mold the next generation of heroes!

48: In the Adventurers' Guild, we see new adventurers as fledgling heroes. They are our champions of tomorrow─our nascent stars of limitless potential!

49: The “New Adventurer” status is applied to all players who have recently begun their adventures in Eorzea.

50: The “New Adventurer” status will be removed from players once they exceed 300 hours of play time, and have completed the main scenario quest “The Coming Dawn.”

51: New adventurers can be identified by the two-leaf symbol (<Gui(77)/>) displayed next to their name.
In the Adventurers' Guild, we see new adventurers as fledgling heroes. They are our champions of tomorrow─our nascent stars of limitless potential!

52: The Adventurers' Guild enlists a cadre of veteran adventurers who stand ready to share with you their accumulated wisdom. These helpful men and women are known as “mentors.”
The “New Adventurer” status is applied to all players who have recently begun their adventures in Eorzea.

53: Eorzea has a thriving population of heroes. Amongst these champions, “mentor” is the title given to veteran players who have chosen to offer their time and experience to help guide new adventurers.
The “New Adventurer” status will be removed from players once they exceed 300 hours of play time, and have completed the main scenario quest “The Coming Dawn.”

61: Mentors can join or leave this channel any time they wish. New adventurers and returners, however, must be invited to join the Novice Network by a mentor.

62: Mentors can extend an invite by targeting a new adventurer or returner, then selecting the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Invite to Novice Network<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> subcommand.

63: The Novice Network can be left at any time by selecting the leaf icon in the log window, then pressing the Leave button in the Novice Network List.
All new adventurers, returners, and mentors can gain access to an exclusive chat channel known as the “Novice Network.”

64: The Novice Network is a place where beginning players can share their experiences and pose questions on adventuring to participating mentors. Be sure to take full advantage of this channel, but also remember to be polite and respectful while doing so!
Mentors can join or leave this channel any time they wish. New adventurers and returners, however, must be invited to join the Novice Network by a mentor.

65: We have found that pairing a new adventurer with a mentor proves advantageous for both. Fledgling heroes blossom swiftly under a veteran's guidance, and the act of teaching serves to reinforce a tested champion's fundamental skills.

Mentors can extend an invite by targeting a new adventurer or returner, then selecting the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Invite to Novice Network<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> subcommand.

66: When a new adventurer or returner forms a party with a mentor, both will earn more experience points from adventuring activities. This experience point bonus applies until level 90.
